english: Cs., tsi-tsi-dzó-ba (tsi tsi dzo ba) Sch. cancer (disease), said to be a Nepalese word.
v. tse-tsé (tse tse).
tsig-tsíg byéd-pa (tsig tsig byed pa) to quarrel, to be at variance Sch.
Ssk. the heart as seat of the intellect, v. Burn. I, 637.
anvil Sch.
num. fig.: 77.
tsú-dai śiṅ (tsu dai shing) n. of a tree Sch.

for ċi-ltar (ci ltar) adv. interrog. and correlat., how, as, rarely occurring in books; Pth.: de gar ꜝgro (de gar 'gro), tsug byed (tsug byed) where she is going, and what she is doing. In W. com. in the form zug (zug), in such combinations as: gá-zug (ga zug) for ċí-tsug (ci tsug), ċi-ltar (ci ltar); *ï-zug* (i zug) or *di-zug* (di zug), and *ă-zug* (a zug) or *dé-zug* (de zug): so; *daṅ de-zug de-zug* (dang de zug de zug) and more of that kind; de-tsug (de tsug) lags (lags) in Lexx.
tsug-tsúg (tsug tsug) the noise of smacking in eating, tsug-tsúg mi bya (tsug tsug mi bya) do not smack Zam.
num. fig.: 107.
Sch.: 1. a small tube. — 2. a little.

tsé-po (tse po), tsél-po (tsel po) a basket carried on the back, dosser, esp. W.; *ċáṅ-tse* (cang tse) or *ċág-tse* (cag tse) a wicker basket, *nyúṅ-tse* (nyung tse) or *nyúg-tse* (nyug tse) a cane basket Ts.; *tsel-ċúg* (tsel cug) the wands used for such a basket; *tsel-ċág* (tsel cag) a broken dosser W.; *tsel-rá* (tsel ra) the frame-work of a basket Cs.; *tsel-lúṅ* (tsel lung) string or strap for carrying it.
tse-tsé (tse tse), tsi-tsé (tsi tse) millet Cs.
1 song, tune Lex. — 2. = tse-ré (tse re).
tseg-tség (tseg tseg), tseg-tség zér-ba (tseg tseg zer ba) to rustle, 'to make a noise like dry hay' Cs.
sharp-pointed, of needles, thorns.
= ċèm-tse (cem tse) small scissors.
a species of female demons Thgr.
tser-tsér (tser tser), tser-tsér byéd-pa (tser tser byed pa) to tremble, shake, quake Sch.
v. tsé-po (tse po).
num. fig.: 137.
Wdṅ., Ssk. n. for the medicinal herb srúb-ka (srub ka); in Ssk. Lexx. no botanical explication is given, but only the notice, that it is a perfume; in Kullu a sweet-scented white lily is called so.

(acc. to one Lex. = उड्डुकटुक which is not to be found; on the other hand Burn. I, 310 gives tsóg-pu-pa (tsog pu pa) = नैषधिक one sitting down) the posture of cowering, squatting, crouching, tsog(-tsog)-pur (tsog(-tsog)-pur) sdód-pa (sdod pa), ꜝdúg-pa ('dug pa) resp. bźúgs-pa (bzhugs pa) Pth., col. *tsoṅ-tsóṅ* (tsong tsong), *tsom-tsóm* (tsom tsom), to cower, squat, crouch; tsóg-pu mi nus (tsog pu mi nus) he cannot even cower, of one sick unto death Thgy.; tsog mi gzúg-pa (tsog mi gzug pa) of a similar sense Sch. — (The version 'to sit on one leg drawn in' Sch., which has also been adopted by Burn., may possibly be founded on a mistake of Sch., who in Cs.'s explanation: 'sitting in a crouching posture upon one's legs', prob. read 'upon one leg').
n. of a place in Eastern Tibet Ma.; tsoṅ-k̇a-pa (tsong kha pa) 1. inhabitant of that place. 2. n. of a celebrated teacher of religion and reformer, about the year 1400.
1. = tsog-tsóg (tsog tsog) v. tsóg-pu (tsog pu). — 2. tsoṅ-tsóṅ-la kúr (tsong tsong la kur) carry it straight W.
v. btson-dóṅ (btson dong).
*tsob-tsób* (tsob tsob), *tsob-tsób-la dúg-ċe* (tsob tsob la dug ce) Ld. to stand or sit in different groups, not in rows.
a five-finger pinch Cs.
v. ꜝtság-pa ('tsag pa); gtság-bu (gtsag bu) also btságs-bu (btsags bu) lancet for bleeding.
1. clean, pure v. gtsáṅ-ba (gtsang ba). — 2. n. of a province in C., where Tasilhunpo is situated; gtsáṅ-pa (gtsang pa) inhabitant of it.
